Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rayonhunte <5769425+rayonhunte@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Firestore rules and enhance race management features

- Updated Firestore rules to allow group admins to manage active member roles.
- Added circuitTimezone property to various request and response types for race management.
- Implemented updateRaceCircuitTimezone function to allow admins to set race timezones.
- Enhanced AdminPage and PicksPage to support circuit timezone selection and display.
- Introduced F1_CIRCUIT_TIMEZONES for user-friendly timezone selection in the Admin interface.

* Enhance AdminPage, DashboardPage, LeaderboardPage, and PicksPage with various improvements

- Added comments to clarify the use of state synchronization in AdminPage and PicksPage.
- Simplified boolean logic for notification preferences in DashboardPage.
- Refactored leaderboard data handling in LeaderboardPage for better readability and performance.
- Updated PicksPage to handle optional race ID and existing picks more robustly.

* Add 'requestedAt' field to Firestore rules for enhanced data tracking

* Enhance Firebase deployment workflow and AdminPage race synchronization

- Added a check to ensure the Firestore rules file exists before deployment in the GitHub Actions workflow.
- Updated the runRaceSync function to return an 'alreadySynced' flag, indicating if a race has already been synced.
- Introduced a new RaceSyncResponse type in AdminPage to handle race synchronization responses.
- Implemented race synchronization functionality in AdminPage, including user notifications for sync status.

* Implement team theme selection and enhance AdminPage functionality

- Replaced the theme toggle button with a dropdown for selecting team themes in ThemeToggle component.
- Introduced a new TeamThemeId type and TEAM_THEMES constant for managing theme options.
- Updated AdminPage to include selectedSeasonYear in the state and added live roster counts fetching logic.
- Enhanced various components with improved styling and accessibility features.
- Added CSS variables for new team themes to support dark mode and improve UI consistency.

* Add messaging functionality and update Firestore rules

- Introduced a new MessagesPage for group messaging, allowing users to send and receive messages with @-mention support.
- Updated Firestore rules to manage message access and permissions for group members.
- Added a function to post system messages to groups upon leaderboard synchronization.
- Enhanced routing and app layout to include the new MessagesPage.

* Remove unused LiveRosterCounts type after merge

Made-with: Cursor

* Add live roster counts fetching functionality in AdminPage

- Introduced a new type LiveRosterCounts to track the number of drivers and constructors.
- Implemented fetchLiveRosterCounts function to retrieve and return the counts from Firestore.
- Added a liveRosterQuery to manage the state of live roster counts in AdminPage.

* Refactor LiveRosterCounts type in AdminPage

- Removed the standalone LiveRosterCounts type and integrated its structure directly into the fetchLiveRosterCounts function's return type.
- Updated the fetchLiveRosterCounts function to streamline the fetching of driver and constructor counts from Firestore.

* fix: use inline return type for fetchLiveRosterCounts to resolve TS2304

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor: simplify group switching logic in GroupsPage

- Removed the dependency on activeGroupId and streamlined the group switching process.
- Introduced a new handleGoToGroup function to manage group navigation and error handling.
- Updated group role display for clarity in the UI.
- Enhanced button text for better user experience.

* Side nav for all viewports, Race Stats page, mobile overflow fixes, How To Use updates

- AppLayout: slide-out side nav only (hamburger + panel); remove horizontal nav
- Add RaceStatsPage (tabs: by race, driver stats) with potential points
- Router: /app/stats route; AppLayout: Stats nav link
- CSS: viewport overflow-x hidden, word-break/wrap, header ellipsis, stats tables mobile-friendly
- HowToUsePage: groups/sign-in, messages, team themes, renumber features

Made-with: Cursor

* Add season schedule importer

* Add season schedule import access

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(3-7): code review fixes — ARIA grid structure, scrollToIndex, test mocks

HIGH: Move role=grid to outer wrapper so role=rowgroup header is nested
inside role=grid per WCAG 2.1 AA grid pattern (AC #4 now fully met)

HIGH: Add rowVirtualizer.scrollToIndex(next, {align:'auto'}) on
ArrowDown/ArrowUp so keyboard-navigated rows scroll into view when
they fall outside the virtual window (AC #8 now fully correct)

MEDIUM: Add scrollToIndex: vi.fn() to both test file virtualizer mocks

commit.message:
feat: Teams notifications + MCP passthrough for agent tools

- Built-in Teams notifier: auto-posts to a Teams chat on task-completed
  and task-failed events. Set teamsNotifyChatId in config to enable.
- MCP passthrough: set mcpConfigPath in config to pass additional MCP
  servers (Teams, email, etc.) to every copilot agent invocation.
- Agents inherit all MCP tools from ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json by default.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pre-warm agency deps before opening WT tabs (same pattern as Forge launcher)

FP's 10s timeout was fragile - too slow on repeat runs, too fast on
slow machines. The Forge launcher (pc-setup/launch-forge-console.ps1)
solves this correctly: run 'agency copilot --version' synchronously
before opening any WT tab. npm install completes once in the launcher
window, then both tabs open simultaneously with no race condition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pipeline section now fully driven by MSX milestones

fetch_msx_data.mjs:
- Added buildUncommittedPipelineList(): generates UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE in
  the new list format directly from MSX milestones, filtered to:
  * Commitment != Committed (Pipeline/Uncommitted/Upside/Best Case/Unknown)
  * Status != Completed/Cancelled
  * Date falls in current month + next 2 months
  * Sorted by month then amount descending (TBD at bottom)
  * Status auto-mapped: Best Case -> on track, Uncommitted/Pipeline -> at risk,
    overdue Not Started -> blocked
  * amount_m converted from monthly USD to \
- generateTerritoryFile() now emits UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE (list format)
  instead of only MILESTONES_BY_MONTH; MILESTONES_BY_MONTH kept as Section 3b
  for full audit trail

generate_report.py:
- add_uncommitted_pipeline_section(): if UNCOMMITTED_PIPELINE is absent or
  empty, falls back to MILESTONES_BY_MONTH and converts it on the fly,
  filtering to uncommitted milestones only — ensures all MSX data shows up
  regardless of which file format the territory uses

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
infra: add Key Vault role assignments to Bicep (IaC-managed)

Bot and Function managed identities' 'Key Vault Secrets User' role
assignments on the KV were manually created and not tracked in IaC.
Adding them with their existing GUIDs ensures:
- They survive any future role assignment audits
- A fresh deployment to a new environment gets them automatically
- The Functions app can always resolve @Microsoft.KeyVault() references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: websockets proxy kwarg compatibility for older versions

Add _ws_connect() wrapper that handles both old (<14) and new websockets
versions — older versions don't support the proxy parameter. Fixes
'create_connection() got an unexpected keyword argument proxy' in HAOS
addon container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
android: force OpenGL RHI + proper NullBackend to fix SIGABRT

- Force QSGRendererInterface::OpenGLRhi before QGuiApplication init.
  Qt 6.9 defaults to Vulkan on Android, which conflicts with Android 16
  HWUI Vulkan renderer on Samsung Xclipse 540, causing SIGABRT in
  hwuiTask0 ~500ms after startup.

- Replace plain QObject null backend with NullBackend class that
  exposes all Q_PROPERTYs (connected, voltage, current, etc.) with
  safe defaults. This prevents QML TypeError crashes when QML bindings
  evaluate before the user connects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove forced toolchain pin from go.mod

Previously, go.mod forced all users and CI to use exactly go1.24.13.
This was unnecessarily restrictive.

The actual minimum Go version required is 1.24.0 (determined by the
dependency on github.com/hajimehoshi/ebiten/v2 v2.9.9). By removing
the toolchain line, users can now use any Go 1.24+ toolchain.

Also cleaned up an unused indirect dependency on opd-ai/wain that was
removed by go mod tidy.

Fixes: PLAN.md item 11 (6b)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Tests][UserEvents] Fix tracee discovery race and improve failure diagnostics (#125345)

https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123442

More diagnostics and better tracee timing in response to the failures
described in
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123442#issuecomment-4025436022

Wait for "Recording started" instead of fixed delay. The 300ms fixed
sleep before launching the tracee was insufficient on ARM64 CI where
record-trace's startup (proc scan + ring buffer setup) took ~1845ms. I
believe the tracee would start in the dead zone between the proc scan
and ring buffer enable, so record-trace never discovered it via mmap
events. Replace the sleep with a ManualResetEventSlim that gates on
record-trace's "Recording started" stdout message, which is printed
after PerfSession::enable succeeds.

Improve failure diagnostics
- Add --log-filter to capture debug-level record-trace diagnostics (mmap
discovery, IPC enablement, nettrace event flushing) while suppressing
noisy modules. The log is written to a file and uploaded to Helix only
on failure.
- Add DumpTraceeEvents to log a summary of all events from the tracee
PID in the nettrace on validation failure, to distinguish "event not
captured" from "event captured with unexpected metadata."
- Add tracee-side console messages confirming event emission completed,
and log the tracee exit code.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redirect build caches to native filesystem for v9fs compat

The bind-mounted /copilot-home uses v9fs which doesn't support
atomic rename(). Zig and Cargo builds fail with AccessDenied.
Fix by setting ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR, ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR, and
CARGO_TARGET_DIR to paths on the native container filesystem.

commit.message:
Fix voice loop stopping between form inputs

- Remove duplicate StartVoiceLoop() from AskCurrentFieldAsync that was
  spawning competing loops on each field advance
- AskCurrentFieldAsync now only speaks the question; voice loop resumes
  listening automatically when SpeakAsync returns
- OnAppearing properly sequences: speak first question → start single loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vm): remove storage_class_name from image-based disk

Harvester provider >=1.7.1 rejects storage_class_name on image-based VM
disks with: 'the storage_class_name of an image can only be defined during
image creation'. The storage class for the root disk is inherited from the
image; it must be set via --image-storage-class at image creation time.

- vm/main.tf: remove storage_class_name from disk block
- vm/variables.tf: remove storage_class_name variable
- harvester-vm.sh: remove --storage-class flag, STORAGE_CLASS variable,
  and storage_class_name line from VM tfvars generation
- AGENTS.md: document the constraint and remove stale convention entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(mc): fix Y-boundary layer shift — surface-at-top detection by material

The previous has_air scan was wrong: when the terrain surface genuinely
sits at the very top of a chunk (surface_voxel_y = max_v.y - 1), all
voxels WITHIN the chunk below it are solid STONE/DIRT — there is no AIR
because the AIR is in the +Y chunk above.  has_air returned false, so
the column was classified as all-solid → surface_y = max_v.y + 2.0 →
Y=44841 rendered nothing for that column.  The +Y chunk (Y=44842) saw
it as all-air (surface below) and also rendered nothing.  Result: the
entire surface of that column was MISSING from both chunks, creating the
'3D printer layer shift' / horizontal cut visible at ~59m.

Fix: check the MATERIAL of the topmost voxel (O(1) instead of O(200)):
  • GRASS / ASPHALT / CONCRETE / WATER → genuine surface at chunk top
    → surface_y = (max_v.y - 1) + 0.5  (render correctly)
  • DIRT / STONE / GRAVEL / anything else → all-solid, terrain above
    → surface_y = max_v.y + 2.0  (no phantom, noise margin ≥ 1.5)

This also removes the O(200) has_air column scan, saving ~180K voxel
reads per chunk load.

Bump: v0.1.73 → v0.1.74

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[tests] Fix crash in BitmapContextTest.CreateAdaptive_2 by computing correct buffer size. (#24862)

The hardcoded 512-byte rendering buffer was far too small for a 256x256
adaptive bitmap context. Replace with the correct size computed from the
adaptive parameters (AlignedBytesPerRow * Height) to avoid SIGSEGV in
CGBitmapContextCreateImage when it tries to read beyond the buffer.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: oh-my-posh install - duplicate label, font --user flag, CONFIG NOT FOUND

Three bugs fixed:

1. Duplicate 'Oh My Posh...' label: line 426 wrote the label, then
   Install-WingetApp wrote it again. Fixed by removing the outer Write-Host
   and handling already-installed case directly without going through the helper.

2. CascadiaCode font install: --user flag does not exist in oh-my-posh 29.x.
   Removed the --user attempt and use LASTEXITCODE to detect success instead
   of string-matching against progress-bar-heavy output.

3. CONFIG NOT FOUND on new machine: 'oh-my-posh get shell-themes-path' is
   not a valid subcommand (returns exit code 70). POSH_THEMES_PATH is empty
   because the Store/WinGet version of oh-my-posh does not set it.
   Fix: download 7 curated themes to ~\.config\oh-my-posh\ at install time
   (skipping any already present), create current-theme.txt if missing, and
   update the profile block to use ~\.config\oh-my-posh with a proper guard
   and Set-Theme helper function for theme switching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix false SENSOR_TAMPERING alerts on generator/transfer buses

RULE_ZERO_METER was firing on BUS-001 (SLACK generator, meterCount=0)
because it had active power but no meter consumption. This is expected
for generator buses. Now only checks buses with meterCount > 0.

commit.message:
feat: browser tool gap-fill — 93 new MCP actions for full CLI parity

Add comprehensive gap-fill handlers to the browser MCP tool:
- HAR: start/drain/export (3 actions)
- Network log: start/drain/summary/stop/export (5 actions)
- WebSocket: start/drain/export/connections (4 actions)
- Cookies file I/O: export/import/delete (3 actions)
- Cookie jar: export/import/clear (3 actions)
- Domain blocking: block/block_category/unblock/stats/list/categories (6 actions)
- Proxy: create_pool/chrome_args/next (3 actions)
- Proxy health: check/check_all/rank/filter (4 actions)
- Request execution: execute/batch (2 actions)
- Fingerprint: apply/detect (2 actions)
- Coverage: js_start/js_stop/css_start/css_report (4 actions)
- Performance: trace_start/trace_stop/metrics/timing/resources (5 actions)
- Workers: list/unregister/info (3 actions)
- Iframes: list/eval/content (3 actions)
- Page watcher: start/drain/stop/state (4 actions)
- Accessibility: tree/element/audit (3 actions)
- Print/PDF: pdf/metrics (2 actions)
- Benchmarks: run/report (2 actions)
- Window: new (1 action)
- Rate limiting: set/stats/reset (3 actions)
- Diff: snapshot/screenshot/url (3 actions)
- Downloads: fetch/clear (2 actions)
- Screenshot diff: compare/regression (2 actions)
- Dialog extended: set_handler/accept/dismiss/history/clear (5 actions)
- Network block: block by type (1 action)
- Highlight: selector (1 action)

All handlers delegate to onecrawl_cdp functions (real CDP, no stubs).
574 tests pass, zero warnings.

commit.message:
consensus: Raise same-height fork switch threshold to 3 peers

Require at least 3 peers (not 2) to agree on an alternate chain before
triggering a same-height fork switch. 1-2 disagreeing peers are still
more likely to be the minority fork, especially when the rest of the
network is temporarily unresponsive to chain-tip queries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use npm ci with package-lock.json in bot Dockerfile

npm install without package-lock.json was resolving a version of
fast-xml-parser that requires fast-xml-builder as a separate package
but wasn't finding it. npm ci with package-lock.json ensures exact
reproducible installs matching the lockfile.
